description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a broad, symmetrical blade on each side, featuring a dark metallic finish with orange-brown accents along the blade edges. The blades are wide and curve outward, forming a crescent shape with pointed tips and a central cutout that adds both visual interest and reduced weight. The handle is straight, angular, and appears to be constructed from a sturdy material, matching the orange-brown color of the blade accents. The axe head is securely mounted to the handle, with no visible fasteners, suggesting a modern or stylized construction. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for display, ceremonial use, or as a prop in urban-themed environments. The surface finish is smooth and hard, with a matte to semi-gloss appearance.
description: A double-headed battle axe with wide, crescent-shaped blades and a straight, angular handle in dark metallic and orange-brown tones. The design is robust, modern, and visually striking.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical blades with central cutouts and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the axe, emphasizing the blade thickness and the straight, angular handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the symmetrical blade design and handle alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the consistent blade and handle thickness.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, angular handle with a rectangular cross-section, likely wood or composite, coated in orange-brown paint, no visible grip texture.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped blades with pointed tips, central cutouts, and a symmetrical, modern design; no beard or poll, edges are broad and slightly curved.
attribute_mounting_style: Modern, integrated mounting with no visible fasteners; blade appears molded or securely fitted to the handle.
